meta.title: Avatar
meta.description: A small image or icon that identifies and personalizes the user within the interface.

mdx:
## Overview

Avatar components can include images or the initials of the user’s first and
last name.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=346-2611&t=WljaKGkdJ1dZn3oM-4)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Size

**Medium** (40px)

Most commonly used in the upper right hand side of the page paired with a
drop-down menu.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal">
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" src="https://thispersondoesnotexist.com/ " size="md" />
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" size="md" /> {/* Shows initials when no image */}
  </Stack>
)
```

**X-Small** (32px)

Use small circle avatars in tighter areas like text fields and dropdown fields
or when it is less important information.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal">
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" src="https://thispersondoesnotexist.com/ " size="xs" />
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" size="xs" /> {/* Shows initials when no image */}
  </Stack>
)
```

**2x-Small** (24px)

Use these in condensed areas like notifications or filters.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal">
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" src="https://thispersondoesnotexist.com/ " size="2xs" />
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" size="2xs" /> {/* Shows initials when no image */}
  </Stack>
)
```

### Initials and images

#### Avatar initials

The default and fallback for avatar graphics is to use initials. Default color
should use Primary, but is open to custom colors.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" />
)
```

overview.mdx:
## Overview

Avatar components can include images or the initials of the user’s first and
last name.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=346-2611&t=WljaKGkdJ1dZn3oM-4)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Size

**Medium** (40px)

Most commonly used in the upper right hand side of the page paired with a
drop-down menu.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal">
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" src="https://thispersondoesnotexist.com/ " size="md" />
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" size="md" /> {/* Shows initials when no image */}
  </Stack>
)
```

**X-Small** (32px)

Use small circle avatars in tighter areas like text fields and dropdown fields
or when it is less important information.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal">
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" src="https://thispersondoesnotexist.com/ " size="xs" />
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" size="xs" /> {/* Shows initials when no image */}
  </Stack>
)
```

**2x-Small** (24px)

Use these in condensed areas like notifications or filters.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="horizontal">
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" src="https://thispersondoesnotexist.com/ " size="2xs" />
    <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" size="2xs" /> {/* Shows initials when no image */}
  </Stack>
)
```

### Initials and images

#### Avatar initials

The default and fallback for avatar graphics is to use initials. Default color
should use Primary, but is open to custom colors.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Avatar firstName="Adam" lastName="Vadam" />
)
```

a11y.mdx: ## Accessibility Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory, cognitive, and physical limitations. ```jsx live const App = () => ( ) ``` ### Accessibility standards - **Non-text content:** Avatars must have a text alternative. Use the `alt` attribute on the `` tag (if the avatar is an image) or through `ARIA` if the avatar is more complex. The alt text should describe the avatar's purpose or what it represents (e.g., "User profile picture," "John Doe's avatar," "Company logo"). If the avatar is purely decorative and doesn't convey any meaningful information, the alt attribute should be empty (`alt=""`). - **Info and relationships:** If the avatar is associated with other information (e.g., a username, profile link), the relationship should be programmatically determinable. This can be achieved using semantic `HTML` (e.g., placing the avatar and username within a single element) or ARIA attributes (e.g., `aria-labelledby` or `aria-describedby`). - **Use of color:** Don't rely on color alone to convey information about the avatar (e.g., using a colored border to indicate status). Any information conveyed by color must also be available through other means. - **Color contrast:** If the avatar contains any text (which is less common), that text must meet minimum contrast ratio requirements. - **Keyboard accessibility:** If the avatar is interactive (e.g., clicking it opens a profile), it must be operable using the keyboard. Users should be able to navigate to the avatar using the Tab key and activate it using the Enter or Space key. - **Link purpose:** If the avatar is a link, its purpose should be clear from the surrounding context or from the avatar's alt text. Avoid generic link text like "Click here." - **Focus visible:** If the avatar is interactive, it should have a visible focus indicator when it receives keyboard focus. - **On focus:** If the avatar is interactive and its appearance changes on focus, the change should not be disorienting or unexpected. - **Name, role, value:** Assistive technologies should be able to correctly interpret the role of the avatar (e.g., `image`, `link`) and its name (from the alt text).
